channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Decline in US student enrolment in India linked to rankings, curriculum rigidity and infrastructure concerns
item.description: The article reports a significant decline in the number of American students choosing India as a study-abroad destination over the past decade. According to data from the Institute of International Ed...

item.title: Nottingham maternity services face scrutiny over unsafe care, poor culture and systemic failures
item.description: A BBC Panorama investigation has revealed serious concerns about maternity services at Nottingham University Hospitals NHS Trust, where families were allegedly failed over more than a decade. The inqu...

item.title: U.S. and Iran Exchange Strikes Amid Ongoing Ceasefire Talks and Regional Tensions
item.description: The United States and Iran have exchanged military strikes over the weekend and into Monday, continuing a pattern of escalating confrontations despite ongoing ceasefire negotiations aimed at ending a ...

item.title: Canadian MAF pilot evacuates exposed family during Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo
item.description: A 30-year-old Canadian pilot originally from Kamloops, British Columbia, successfully carried out a medical evacuation mission in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) after an American doctor te...

item.title: PNP confirms leads in ongoing search for Senator Bato dela Rosa amid ICC warrant
item.description: The Philippine National Police (PNP) has confirmed that it has received information and possible leads regarding the whereabouts of Senator Ronald “Bato” dela Rosa, who is currently being sought in co...

item.title: Atiku Abubakar Secures ADC Ticket for 2027 Presidential Race Amid Challenges
item.description: Atiku Abubakar don carry the ADC ticket for 2027 election, marking him sixth serious attempt to become Nigeria president. Na man wey don dey pursue this position since 1993, from SDP to PDP, APC, and ...

item.title: APC defends Tinubu reforms, says confidence in economy is rising
item.description: The All Progressives Congress (APC) has defended the economic reforms introduced by President Bola Tinubu, stating that Nigerians are becoming more confident in the country’s direction under his admin...

item.title: Local Sudanese responders warn of neglect amid ongoing civil war and unequal global humanitarian attention
item.description: Sudan’s civil war, which began in 2021 following a power struggle between the Sudanese Armed Forces (SAF) led by Abdel Fattah al-Burhan and the Rapid Support Forces (RSF) led by Mohamed Hamdan Dagalo,...

item.title: Court considers virtual testimony in long-running PRASA rail theft trial as key witness remains in Dubai
item.description: The fraud and theft trial involving a former PRASA manager and a Cape Town businessman has been further delayed due to a dispute over whether a key state witness should be allowed to testify virtually...

item.title: US 250th anniversary concerts spark cultural satire around Trump and Milli Vanilli comparison
item.description: The article reflects on the satirical and cultural complexity surrounding preparations for the United States’ 250th anniversary celebrations, particularly a series of planned concerts linked to the co...

item.title: Utumishi Girls Academy dormitory fire in Gilgil leaves 16 students dead, video reveals extensive damage
item.description: Families across Kenya are mourning after a tragic fire at Utumishi Girls Academy in Gilgil, Nakuru County, which claimed the lives of 16 students and left several others injured. The incident occurred...

item.title: Authorities investigate burned mail-in ballots and election site vandalism in Los Angeles ahead of primary election
item.description: Los Angeles County election officials are investigating two separate incidents of election-related vandalism reported just days before the June 2 primary election. In downtown Los Angeles, election wo...

item.title: Six practical cooling tools to keep outdoor food and drinks safe in summer heat
item.description: This article highlights practical tools designed to keep food and drinks safely chilled during outdoor summer gatherings, where warm temperatures can quickly increase the risk of foodborne illness. It...

item.title: Sarah Martins reacts to Yul Edochie’s remarks about his ex-wife during podcast interview
item.description: Nollywood actress Sarah Martins has reacted strongly to comments made by actor Yul Edochie regarding his former wife, May Edochie. Yul had appeared on The Honest Bunch Podcast where he suggested that ...

item.title: Darren Till wins BKFC debut with one-punch knockout over Aaron Chalmers
item.description: In his BKFC debut, former UFC contender Darren Till faced Aaron Chalmers in a high-stakes bare-knuckle boxing match at BKFC 90. The fight began with Chalmers taking control, landing a sharp left hook ...

item.title: Tanzania plans stricter regulations on shisha amid concerns over drug misuse
item.description: The Government of Tanzania is preparing new legislation aimed at tightening control over the use of shisha following growing concerns that the product is being misused for consuming narcotic substance...

item.title: Meteor Breaks Apart Over Northeastern US, Producing Significant Explosion
item.description: A meteor traveling at over 120,000 kilometers per hour exploded over the northeastern United States on Saturday, creating a blast equivalent to 300 tons of TNT. NASA confirmed the fireball disintegrat...

item.title: NCR Secures 19th Consecutive Overall Title at 2026 Palarong Pambansa
item.description: The 2026 Palarong Pambansa, held in Agusan del Sur, concluded on May 31 with the National Capital Region (NCR) once again dominating the national school-based sports competition. NCR secured its 19th ...

item.title: President Ruto Launches Development Projects in Northern Kenya Ahead of Madaraka Day
item.description: President William Ruto started a three-day tour of northern Kenya on Sunday, visiting Wajir County as part of preparations for the Madaraka Day celebrations in Wajir town. His first stop was Wajir Sou...

item.title: Federal workers’ forum threatens nationwide strike over wage disputes and rising insecurity
item.description: The Federal Workers Forum (FWF) has issued a strong warning that it may mobilise federal civil servants for an indefinite nationwide strike over unresolved wage issues and worsening insecurity in Nige...
